source_context: Web design and SEO agency monetizing through B2B client acquisition for businesses in Kenya
central_search_intent: Professional website design and SEO services in Kenya — helping businesses rank on Google, attract customers, and grow online
topical_border_definition: INSIDE BORDER: Website design, web development, e-commerce, SEO (technical, on-page, local, content), website maintenance, website speed, mobile optimization, Google Business Profile, Kenya-specific digital factors (M-Pesa, KRA, mobile-first). OUTER BORDER: Digital marketing, branding, domain/hosting, content marketing, Google Ads, Kenya business landscape, digital economy. EXCLUDED: Standalone social media management, business consulting, offline marketing, accounting, legal services, HR.
note_on_score: Score is 0 because no existing content has been audited. This map defines what MUST be built.

core_section.main_attribute_rationale: Web design CREATES digital presence. SEO GROWS digital presence. Both services unify under this single deliverable — making 'digital presence' the main attribute connecting source context to central entity.

All attributes must connect to business outcome: lead generation, customer trust, revenue.

tier_2_topics.attribute_focus: Website Creation — industry-specific design for Kenyan medical clinics, dental practices, and healthcare providers
tier_2_topics.macro_context: What a Kenyan medical or dental clinic website needs — appointment booking, doctor profiles, service descriptions, NHIF information, and the trust signals that Kenyan patients require before choosing a healthcare provider
tier_2_topics.eav_depth_required: Medical/dental website Kenya attributes: appointment booking system (online booking vs WhatsApp booking — Kenya patient preference), doctor and dentist profile pages (qualifications, Kenya Medical Practitioners & Dentists Council registration number, specializations), service pages (procedures with patient-friendly descriptions, pricing transparency for self-pay vs NHIF patients), NHIF acceptance display (critical conversion signal for Kenyan patients), location pages (map, parking, operating hours including Saturday hours — important for Kenyan clinics), emergency contact prominence, before/after gallery for dental (patient consent compliance), lab and diagnostic services catalog, telemedicine option (growing post-COVID in Kenya), patient education content for SEO (dental hygiene, preventive care — high-volume informational queries in Kenya).

question_formats: How does [X] work for Kenyan businesses?
Why does [X] matter for a business in Kenya?
How long does [X] take for a Kenyan website?
What is [X] and how does it affect Google rankings in Kenya?
Which [X] is best for Kenyan [industry] businesses?
predicate_distributions: rank (rank on Google, rank higher, rank for)
grow (grow online, grow visibility, grow traffic)
build (build a website, build authority, build presence)
optimize (optimize for search, optimize for mobile, optimize content)
appear (appear on Google, appear in search results)
macro_semantics.heading_vector_pattern: H1: [Outcome/Benefit] in Kenya — [What We Do]. H2: [Specific aspect] for [Kenyan context/audience]. H3: [Precise entity or attribute with Kenya-specific depth]. First paragraph word: answers directly (e.g., 'Website design in Kenya costs...', 'Local SEO works by...') — never rhetoric ('In today's digital world...').

tier_2_topics.attribute_focus: Minor attribute — foundational infrastructure of a Kenyan business website
tier_2_topics.macro_context: How to register a domain name for a Kenyan business — .co.ke vs .com, registrars, pricing, and what domain choice signals to Kenyan customers and Google
tier_2_topics.eav_depth_required: .co.ke domain attributes: KENIC (Kenya Network Information Centre) as registry, accredited registrars in Kenya (Truehost, Kenya Website Experts, Safaricom, Web Hosting Kenya), .co.ke vs .com vs .africa for Kenyan businesses, domain pricing (annual cost comparison), domain privacy/WHOIS protection, DNS management, domain transfer process, expired domain acquisition for SEO, ccTLD benefit for local search ranking in Kenya.
tier_2_topics.bridge_to_core: Linked bridge to Website Design for Businesses in Kenya — domain choice is the first step in building a professional Kenyan business website.

tier_2_topics.attribute_focus: Minor attribute — server infrastructure that affects Kenyan website performance
tier_2_topics.macro_context: Hosting options for Kenyan business websites — local vs international, speed implications, reliability, cost, and what to look for when choosing a host for a Kenya-based website
tier_2_topics.eav_depth_required: Kenya hosting attributes: local providers (Truehost Kenya, Kenya Website Experts, Safaricom Business, Web4Africa), international with African POPs (SiteGround, Cloudflare, AWS af-south-1 Cape Town), hosting types (shared, VPS, cloud, dedicated), cPanel vs Plesk management, uptime SLA for Kenya market, server location impact on TTFB for Kenyan visitors, SSL inclusion, email hosting integration, bandwidth for Kenyan traffic volumes, backup policies, customer support in Kenyan time zone (EAT UTC+3).
tier_2_topics.bridge_to_core: Linked bridge to Website Speed Optimization Kenya — hosting choice is the primary determinant of server response time (TTFB) for Kenyan visitors.

tier_2_topics.attribute_focus: Minor attribute — paid search complement to organic SEO for Kenyan businesses
tier_2_topics.macro_context: How Google Ads works for Kenyan businesses — campaign setup, budget, bidding, and how it complements SEO as an immediate traffic source
tier_2_topics.eav_depth_required: Google Ads Kenya attributes: campaign types (Search, Display, Shopping, YouTube, Performance Max), Kenyan keyword bid prices by industry (lower CPCs than US/UK — KSh data), audience targeting (Nairobi, Mombasa, county-level geotargeting), ad copy compliance (Google Ads policies for Kenya), billing in KES vs USD, Kenya conversion tracking setup (GA4, Google Ads tag), remarketing for Kenyan website visitors, landing page quality score factors, Google Ads vs Facebook Ads for Kenya — comparative ROI data.
tier_2_topics.bridge_to_core: Linked bridge to Landing Page Design Kenya and SEO Services in Kenya — Google Ads needs optimized landing pages; SEO and Google Ads together dominate both organic and paid search real estate in Kenya.

tier_2_topics.attribute_focus: Minor attribute — blog and article content that builds organic traffic for Kenyan business websites
tier_2_topics.macro_context: How Kenyan businesses use blog content and educational articles to attract organic traffic, build trust, and support SEO
tier_2_topics.eav_depth_required: Content marketing Kenya attributes: content formats (how-to guides, listicles, industry reports for Kenyan markets, case studies of Kenyan businesses), editorial calendar planning for Kenya, content distribution channels (organic search, LinkedIn Kenya, Twitter/X Kenya, WhatsApp groups), content length benchmarks for Kenyan search ranking, evergreen vs trending content for Kenya, content ROI measurement (traffic to lead attribution), outsourcing content writing (Kenyan freelancers vs content agencies), pillar page and cluster content structure for Kenya niches.
tier_2_topics.bridge_to_core: Linked bridge to Content Strategy for Kenyan Websites and SEO Services in Kenya — content marketing is the execution of content strategy and directly fuels SEO.

tier_2_topics.attribute_focus: Minor attribute — visual identity that precedes and informs website design for Kenyan businesses

tier_2_topics.bridge_to_core: Linked bridge to Website Design for Businesses in Kenya — brand identity is the visual foundation that website design implements and extends.

tier_2_topics.attribute_focus: Minor attribute — measuring and understanding website performance for Kenyan businesses
tier_2_topics.macro_context: How Kenyan businesses track website performance — setting up GA4, interpreting traffic data, and using analytics to make business decisions
tier_2_topics.eav_depth_required: Analytics Kenya attributes: GA4 setup (property creation, data stream, measurement protocol), key reports for Kenyan businesses (audience location filter for Kenya, device breakdown — mobile dominance confirmation, traffic source analysis — direct, organic, social, referral), conversion tracking setup (contact form submissions, phone call clicks, WhatsApp link clicks, M-Pesa payment completions), Google Search Console integration (Kenya performance filter, query data, CTR optimization), Looker Studio dashboard for client reporting, privacy compliance (Kenya Data Protection Act 2019 and analytics cookie consent).
tier_2_topics.bridge_to_core: Linked bridge to SEO Services in Kenya — analytics is the measurement layer that proves SEO results and informs strategy for Kenyan websites.

tier_3_topics.attribute_focus: Deep contextual bridge — market landscape that creates the demand for e-commerce website development in Kenya
tier_3_topics.macro_context: The state of e-commerce in Kenya — market size, growth, consumer behavior, major players, and what it means for businesses going online
tier_3_topics.eav_depth_required: Kenya e-commerce attributes: market size (Kenya e-commerce revenue data, growth rate), major platforms (Jumia Kenya, Jiji Kenya, Kilimall), consumer behavior (M-Pesa payment preference, cash-on-delivery still significant, trust barriers for online shopping), product categories with highest online demand in Kenya (electronics, fashion, beauty, groceries — Naivas Online, QuickMart), delivery infrastructure (Sendy, Glovo, Bolt Food for last-mile), internet penetration by county, digital literacy by age bracket in Kenya.
tier_3_topics.bridge_to_core: Linkless bridge to E-commerce Website Development Kenya — consistent positioning of Kenya's e-commerce growth as the reason businesses need a professional online store.

tier_3_topics.topic: Kenya Digital Economy and ICT Landscape
tier_3_topics.attribute_focus: Deep contextual bridge — national digital context that gives meaning to web design and SEO in Kenya
tier_3_topics.macro_context: How Kenya's digital economy creates the environment in which Tupae Studio operates — internet penetration, smartphone adoption, government digital initiatives, and the opportunity for Kenyan businesses online
tier_3_topics.eav_depth_required: Kenya ICT attributes: internet penetration (CA Kenya reports — 2024 figures), mobile subscription data (Safaricom, Airtel, Telkom market share), Silicon Savannah reputation (Nairobi as Africa's tech hub), government digital initiatives (Huduma Namba, e-government, eCitizen platform), Kenya ICT Authority regulations for websites, data center infrastructure (EAOX — East Africa Internet Exchange, CDC Kenya), broadband infrastructure (TEAMS cable, SEACOM, EASSy undersea cables), Konza Technopolis development, university ICT programs driving tech talent.
tier_3_topics.bridge_to_core: Linkless bridge to SEO Services in Kenya and Website Design for Businesses in Kenya — positions Tupae Studio as experts operating within Kenya's growing digital economy.

tier_3_topics.topic: Small and Medium Enterprise (SME) Digital Adoption Kenya
tier_3_topics.attribute_focus: Deep contextual bridge — the primary customer segment for Tupae Studio and their digital challenges
tier_3_topics.macro_context: The state of digital adoption among Kenyan SMEs — barriers to going online, what drives SME website investment, and how digital presence changes SME fortunes
representative_queries: how to take business online Kenya
online presence for small business Kenya
tier_3_topics.eav_depth_required: Kenya SME digital attributes: SME definition in Kenya (Kenya National Bureau of Statistics criteria), number of registered SMEs in Kenya, percentage with websites vs without, barriers to website adoption (cost misconceptions, technical complexity perception, ROI doubt), government support programs (MSEA, Kenya ICT Board digital programs for SMEs), KEPSA (Kenya Private Sector Alliance) digital initiatives, success stories of Kenyan SMEs that grew through digital, ROI of website for Kenyan SMEs by sector (hospitality, retail, professional services).
tier_3_topics.bridge_to_core: Linked bridge to Website Design for Businesses in Kenya — SME digital adoption context leads directly to the case for professional website design.

tier_3_topics.topic: Kenya Data Protection Act and Website Compliance
tier_3_topics.attribute_focus: Deep contextual bridge — legal requirement context affecting every Kenyan business website
tier_3_topics.macro_context: What the Kenya Data Protection Act (2019) requires of business websites — privacy policies, cookie consent, data handling, and compliance for Kenyan website owners
tier_3_topics.eav_depth_required: KDPA website attributes: Kenya Data Protection Act 2019 key provisions for websites (data controller registration with ODPC, lawful basis for data collection, privacy notice requirements), cookie consent implementation for Kenyan websites, privacy policy required components under KDPA, data breach notification obligations (72-hour rule), cross-border data transfer restrictions, penalties for non-compliance (KSh. 5 million fine or imprisonment), Office of the Data Protection Commissioner (ODPC) registration process, GDPR comparison for Kenyan businesses serving international clients.
tier_3_topics.bridge_to_core: Linked bridge to Website Design for Businesses in Kenya and Corporate Website Design Kenya — compliance is a design requirement, not an afterthought, for Kenyan business websites.

tier_3_topics.attribute_focus: Deep contextual bridge — social platforms that drive traffic to Kenyan business websites
tier_3_topics.macro_context: How Kenyan businesses use social media to drive traffic to their websites — Facebook, Instagram, TikTok, LinkedIn, and WhatsApp in the Kenyan market context
tier_3_topics.eav_depth_required: Kenya social media attributes: platform penetration (Facebook still #1 by active users in Kenya, TikTok fastest growing, Instagram for visual brands, LinkedIn for B2B Kenya, Twitter/X for news and commentary), WhatsApp Business features for Kenyan businesses (catalog, broadcast lists, quick replies — most converted channel in Kenya), Meta Ads for Kenyan audience targeting, TikTok for Business Kenya, content formats by platform (Reels, Stories, posts), social proof → website traffic funnel for Kenya, social media profile optimization to drive website clicks, link-in-bio strategy for Kenya.
tier_3_topics.bridge_to_core: Linked bridge to Digital Marketing Services Kenya and Website Design for Businesses in Kenya — social media is the traffic driver; the website is the conversion engine.

coverage_gaps.topic: NGO and Non-Profit Website Design Kenya
coverage_gaps.reason: Kenya has thousands of registered NGOs. NGO website requirements (donor pages, programme pages, impact reporting, grant compliance, NGO Board registration display) are distinct from commercial business websites and warrant a dedicated node. Low competition in search.

Swahili search behavior in Kenya is an underexplored but growing area. A dedicated node on Swahili SEO strategy (Swahili keyword research, hreflang for Swahili pages, Swahili content optimization) would give Tupae Studio a unique competitive advantage.

coverage_gaps.topic: Link Building and Digital PR for Kenyan Websites
coverage_gaps.reason: Off-page SEO (earning backlinks from Kenyan media, industry associations, government portals) is a critical ranking factor not covered in the current map. Business Daily Africa, Nation Media Group, Standard Group digital — these are natural link targets for Kenyan businesses.

coverage_gaps.topic: Schema Markup for Kenyan Local Businesses
coverage_gaps.reason: Schema markup is touched in Technical SEO Audit but deserves its own depth-first node — types, Kenya-specific attributes (accepting M-Pesa in schema), rich result opportunities in Kenyan SERPs.

WhatsApp is Kenya's primary business communication channel. A dedicated node on WhatsApp Business API integration with websites (click-to-WhatsApp buttons, WhatsApp catalog integration, WhatsApp Business API for larger businesses) is a major gap given its conversion importance.

coverage_gaps.topic: KRA iTax and eTIMS Compliance for E-commerce
coverage_gaps.reason: Kenya Revenue Authority's eTIMS (Electronic Tax Invoice Management System) is now mandatory for businesses above a threshold. E-commerce websites must integrate eTIMS compliance. This is a highly specific, low-competition topic with high commercial intent.

vastness_depth_momentum.vastness_assessment: The topical map now has 6 Tier 1 Core topics, 18 Tier 2 Core topics (including 6 confirmed industry verticals from business plan), 3 Tier 1 Outer topics, 6 Tier 2 Outer topics, and 5 Tier 3 Outer topics — totaling 38 content nodes. Industry verticals (Industrial/Machinery, Medical/Dental, Law/Accounting, Real Estate, Hospitality, Schools) are now confirmed niches with dedicated pages — not just coverage gaps. The map is wide and niche-deep. Phase 2 expansion: NGO/non-profit websites, and deeper geographic targeting (Mombasa, Kisumu, Nakuru city-specific pages).
vastness_depth_momentum.depth_assessment: Depth is strong at the framework level — each node has Kenya-specific EAV requirements. Actual coverage depth score is 0% (no content published). The custom-built website positioning (vs WordPress) is a strategic differentiator that must permeate all content: performance data, security comparisons, Core Web Vitals advantages. This is not just a service feature — it is a topical authority claim that changes the macro semantics of the entire site.
vastness_depth_momentum.momentum_recommendation: Priority order: Core Tier 1 service pages first (6 pages, Month 1) — these are the pages clients land on from a pitch demo link or Google search. Then industry vertical pages (Month 2) — these directly support the 6 target niches in the business plan and allow Tupae Studio to build demo sites for each niche with an SEO-optimised case study page. Then Core Tier 2 (Month 3-4). Outer Section (Month 5-6). Minimum momentum: 2 substantial pages/week at 1,500+ words with Kenya-specific depth.
vastness_depth_momentum.priority_recommendation: DEPTH first, then MOMENTUM. The custom-built positioning plus Kenya specificity is the topical border that generic agencies cannot cross. Every page must answer queries that a WordPress-using web agency in Nairobi cannot answer credibly. Industry vertical pages are strategically critical — they match the business plan's prospecting targets and enable the 'free demo site' pitch strategy: each niche page becomes both an SEO asset and a sales tool.
